name: Du lac et des coteaux du Lizet — loop — running from Montesquiou
name_original: Du lac et des coteaux du Lizet — boucle — course à pied depuis Montesquiou
region_intro: Jogging around Barrage Du Lizet offers routes that explore the area's characteristic lake and surrounding hillsides. The terrain primarily features gentle slopes and paths that wind through the local countryside, often alongside the water. This region provides a mix of open views and more sheltered sections, suitable for various running preferences. Best jogging routes around Barrage Du Lizet The most popular jogging route is Du lac et des coteaux du Lizet — loop — running from Montesquiou, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail that takes 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the lake and surrounding hillsides. Another top favourite among local runners is Lac du Lizet – Lac du Lizet loop from Estipouy, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This route provides a longer circuit around the lake, ideal for extended running sessions. Local runners also love the Lac du Lizet loop from L'Isle-de-Noé, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside with gentle elevation changes, often completed in about 1 hour 0 minutes. Jogging around Barrage Du Lizet is defined by lake loops, rolling hills, and paths through the local countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter, easier runs to longer, moderate challenges. The routes in Barrage Du Lizet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Barrage Du Lizet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Barrage Du Lizet?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Barrage Du Lizet, offering a variety of distances and terrains. The komoot community has explored 33 routes in total.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Barrage Du Lizet?
A: Yes, while most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy running route available. The area's terrain generally features gentle slopes and paths, making it accessible for those new to running or looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Are the running trails around Barrage Du Lizet suitable for families?
A: Many of the routes around Barrage Du Lizet, with their gentle slopes and scenic lake views, are suitable for families. Consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197055123/du-lac-et-des-coteaux-du-lizet-loop-running-from-montesquiou" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Du lac et des coteaux du Lizet — loop — running from Montesquiou&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4 km loop, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running paths in Barrage Du Lizet?
A: Generally, the rural trails around Barrage Du Lizet are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and to clean up after them. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there circular running routes around Barrage Du Lizet?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac du Lizet – Lac du Lizet loop from Estipouy&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 12.1 km circuit, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35793134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac du Lizet loop from L'Isle-de-Noé&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.6 km loop through the countryside.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails near Barrage Du Lizet?
A: Parking is typically available in the nearby villages such as Montesquiou, Estipouy, L'Isle-de-Noé, or Mirande, which serve as common starting points for many of the running routes. Look for designated parking areas or roadside spots where permitted.

Q: What kind of views or points of interest can I expect on the running routes?
A: The running routes around Barrage Du Lizet offer picturesque views of the lake itself, as well as the surrounding rolling hills and local countryside. You'll experience a mix of open vistas and more sheltered sections, often alongside the water. The area's natural beauty is a key highlight.

Q: What is the best season for running in Barrage Du Lizet?
A: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for running around Barrage Du Lizet, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or evenings, but be prepared for warmer weather. Winter running is possible, but trails might be damp or muddy.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Barrage Du Lizet?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running trails around Barrage Du Lizet, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the tranquil lake loops, the scenic rolling hills, and the peaceful paths through the local countryside, noting the variety suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any less crowded running routes around Barrage Du Lizet?
A: While the main lake loops are popular, exploring routes that venture slightly further into the local countryside or starting your run earlier in the day can help you find quieter paths. Routes originating from smaller villages like L'Isle-de-Noé might offer a more serene experience.

Q: Are there cafes or amenities near the running paths?
A: You'll find cafes and other amenities in the nearby towns and villages such as Montesquiou, Estipouy, L'Isle-de-Noé, and Mirande. These are good spots to refuel before or after your run, as the trails themselves are primarily natural and rural.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in this area?
A: Running durations vary significantly based on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197055123/du-lac-et-des-coteaux-du-lizet-loop-running-from-montesquiou" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Du lac et des coteaux du Lizet — loop — running from Montesquiou&lt;/a&gt; takes about 29 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Mirande&lt;/a&gt; is typically completed in around 39 minutes.
tips: East of Montesquiou and west of Isle-de-Noé, Lac du Lizet was created to meet the water needs of Gascogne. It welcomes many birds and its hills overlooking it are among the richest and remarkable in the Gers.
